title: Exotic Winey Ethiopian Pork Back Ribs Kitfo
description: A winey, crisp, and light Ethiopian dish featuring pork back ribs with a yuzu-cure, this kosher-friendly kitfo is a unique twist on traditional Ethiopian cuisine. The combination of flavors and textures creates a truly exotic experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together yuzu juice, red wine, berbere spice, garlic, ginger, and olive oil.
Add the pork back ribs to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the ribs from the marinade and season with salt.
Place the ribs on a baking sheet lined with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Increase the oven temperature to 425 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes.
Remove the ribs from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the ribs with a side of injera bread or other traditional Ethiopian flatbread.
